title: config.ts: reduce default IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES from 30 to 15
description:
One-line change in packages/opencode-router/src/config.ts:
  Change: Number(process.env.IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES ?? 30)
  To:     Number(process.env.IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES ?? 15)
  Independent of all other tasks — can land first.

title: router-app api.ts: update types + add resume, terminate, suggestBranch
description:
File: packages/opencode-router-app/src/api.ts
Depends on: backend tasks opencode-1.3.6/7/8/9

Changes:
1. Session interface:
   - state union: "creating" | "running" | "stopped"
   - add lastActivity: string | null
   - add idleTimeoutMinutes: number

2. Add resumeSession:
   export async function resumeSession(hash: string): Promise<{ hash: string; url: string; state: string }>
   → POST /api/sessions/:hash/resume
   → throws on non-ok response

3. Add terminateSession:
   export async function terminateSession(hash: string): Promise<void>
   → DELETE /api/sessions/:hash
   → throws on non-ok response

4. Add suggestBranch:
   export async function suggestBranch(repoUrl: string): Promise<{ branch: string }>
   → GET /api/sessions/suggest-branch?repoUrl=<encoded>
   → throws on non-ok response

title: router-app app.tsx: stopped sessions, idle countdown, Resume + Terminate
description:
File: packages/opencode-router-app/src/app.tsx Depends on: opencode-1.3.10 Changes: 1. Session card rendering — replace the single with conditional rendering: - state "running" or "creating": keep wrapping the card - state "stopped": render a plain
(no link — no pod to navigate to) 2. Status line text (the second

below the repo name): - "creating" → "starting…" - "running" → "stops in ~Xm" where X = Math.max(0, Math.round( (new Date(session.lastActivity!).getTime() + session.idleTimeoutMinutes * 60000 - Date.now()) / 60000 )) - "stopped" → session.lastActivity ? "stopped " + Math.round((Date.now() - new Date(session.lastActivity).getTime()) / 60000) + "m ago" : "stopped" 3. Add Resume button (only when state === "stopped"): handleResume: calls resumeSession(hash) → setPhase({ kind: "creating", hash, url }) On error: show inline error message on that card. 4. Add Terminate button (always visible on each card): handleTerminate: optimistically remove session from list signal, call terminateSession(hash). On error: reload full session list via loadSessions(). No native confirm() dialog — use an inline "Are you sure?" toggle state on the card (a confirmingHash signal: string | null) to avoid blocking the UI.

title: router-app setup-form.tsx: auto-fill branch from suggest-branch endpoint
description:
File: packages/opencode-router-app/src/setup-form.tsx
Depends on: opencode-1.3.10

Changes:
1. Add signal: const [branchTouched, setBranchTouched] = createSignal(false)
   Set to true in the branch TextField onChange handler (user typed something).

2. Add effect that fires when repoUrl() changes:
   createEffect(() => {
     const url = repoUrl().trim()
     if (!GIT_URL_PATTERN.test(url)) return
     if (branchTouched()) return  // do not overwrite user input
     suggestBranch(url)
       .then(({ branch }) => { if (!branchTouched()) setBranch(branch) })
       .catch(() => {})  // silent fail — branch field stays empty
   })

3. Show a subtle loading indicator while suggestion is in-flight:
   Add signal: const [suggesting, setSuggesting] = createSignal(false)
   Wrap suggestBranch call with setSuggesting(true/false).
   Add placeholder or helper text: suggesting() ? "generating…" : "main"

title: deployment: add delete verb on PVCs to RBAC role
description:
File: deployment/homelab/src/index.ts
Independent — can land at any time.

Find the persistentvolumeclaims rule in the Role resource (around line 107):
  {
    apiGroups: [""],
    resources: ["persistentvolumeclaims"],
    verbs: ["get", "list", "create"],
  },

Change to:
  {
    apiGroups: [""],
    resources: ["persistentvolumeclaims"],
    verbs: ["get", "list", "create", "delete"],
  }

Without this, terminateSession will receive a 403 from the k8s API when attempting
to delete a PVC.

title: fix branch handling: sourceBranch (user input) vs sessionBranch (auto-generated, display-only)
dev-proxy: auto kubectl port-forward for local dev
pod-manager: stopped state + listUserSessions from PVCs
description:
File: packages/opencode-router/src/pod-manager.ts

1. Extend PodState type:
   export type PodState = "none" | "creating" | "running" | "stopped"

2. Extend SessionInfo interface with two new fields:
   lastActivity: string | null   // ISO timestamp from pod annotation; null when stopped with no record
   idleTimeoutMinutes: number    // from config.idleTimeoutMinutes

3. Rewrite listUserSessions to scan PVCs as source of truth:
   - List PVCs with labelSelector: app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=opencode-router
   - Filter by ANNOTATION_USER_EMAIL === email
   - List ALL pods once (same label selector), build a Map<hash, V1Pod> to avoid N+1 calls
   - For each PVC, look up its hash from labels[LABEL_SESSION_HASH]:
       - No pod found                          → state = "stopped"
       - Pod found, phase=Running + podIP set  → state = "running"
       - Pod found, otherwise                  → state = "creating"
   - lastActivity: read from pod annotation if pod exists; null otherwise
   - Edge: skip PVCs whose hash label is missing or not 12 hex chars

title: pod-manager: add terminateSession (delete pod + PVC)
description:
File: packages/opencode-router/src/pod-manager.ts
Depends on: opencode-1.3.2 (stopped state)

Add export:
  export async function terminateSession(hash: string, email: string): Promise<void>

Steps:
  1. Read PVC by pvcName(hash). If 404 → throw { code: 404 }.
  2. Check PVC annotations[ANNOTATION_USER_EMAIL] === email. If mismatch → throw { code: 403 }.
  3. Delete pod by podName(hash) — ignore 404 (already gone for stopped sessions).
  4. Delete PVC by pvcName(hash).
     Note: if pod is still terminating and has the volume mounted, k8s will queue the PVC
     deletion via finalizer — this is acceptable, do not retry or poll.
  5. activityThrottle.delete(hash)

title: pod-manager: add resumeSession (recreate pod for stopped session)
description:
File: packages/opencode-router/src/pod-manager.ts
Depends on: opencode-1.3.2

Add export:
  export async function resumeSession(session: SessionKey): Promise<string>

Steps:
  1. Compute hash = getSessionHash(session.email, session.repoUrl, session.branch)
  2. Read PVC by pvcName(hash). If 404 → throw { code: 404 } (session was terminated).
  3. Call ensurePod(session) — already idempotent; creates pod if absent, no-op if running.
  4. Return hash.

The caller (api.ts) returns { hash, url, state: "creating" } — the frontend reuses
the existing LoadingScreen polling loop to wait for Running.

title: pod-manager: add suggestBranch (collision-safe human-id name)
api.ts: DELETE /api/sessions/:hash (terminate)
description:
File: packages/opencode-router/src/api.ts
Depends on: opencode-1.3.3

Import terminateSession from pod-manager.

Add route handler (place BEFORE the existing GET /api/sessions/:hash handler):
  Pattern: /^\/api\/sessions\/([a-f0-9]{12})$/ + method DELETE
  Steps:
    1. Extract hash from match[1]
    2. Call terminateSession(hash, email)
    3. On success → json(res, 204, {}) — actually res.writeHead(204).end()
    4. Catch { code: 404 } → json(res, 404, { error: "session not found" })
    5. Catch { code: 403 } → json(res, 403, { error: "forbidden" })

title: api.ts: POST /api/sessions/:hash/resume
description:
File: packages/opencode-router/src/api.ts
Depends on: opencode-1.3.4

Import resumeSession from pod-manager.
Also import k8sApi or a helper to read PVC annotations — or add a helper
getPVCSession(hash): Promise<SessionKey> to pod-manager that reads PVC annotations
and returns { email, repoUrl, branch }.

Add route handler:
  Pattern: /^\/api\/sessions\/([a-f0-9]{12})\/resume$/ + method POST
  Steps:
    1. Extract hash from match[1]
    2. Read PVC to reconstruct SessionKey (email, repoUrl, branch from annotations)
       Verify PVC annotations[ANNOTATION_USER_EMAIL] === email → 403 if mismatch
       PVC not found → 404
    3. Call resumeSession({ email, repoUrl, branch })
    4. Return json(res, 200, { hash, url: sessionUrl(hash, req), state: "creating" })

Note: "creating" is always safe to return even if pod was already running — the
LoadingScreen will poll and redirect once Running.

title: api.ts: GET /api/sessions/suggest-branch?repoUrl=
description:
File: packages/opencode-router/src/api.ts
Depends on: opencode-1.3.5

Import suggestBranch from pod-manager.

Add route handler (place BEFORE /api/sessions/:hash handlers — belt-and-suspenders
even though "suggest-branch" cannot match the 12-hex-char hash regex):
  Pattern: url === "/api/sessions/suggest-branch" + method GET
  Steps:
    1. Parse repoUrl from query string: new URL(url, "http://x").searchParams.get("repoUrl")
    2. If !repoUrl → json(res, 400, { error: "repoUrl is required" })
    3. Call suggestBranch(email, repoUrl)
    4. Return json(res, 200, { branch })

title: api.ts: include lastActivity + idleTimeoutMinutes in session list and get
description:
File: packages/opencode-router/src/api.ts
Depends on: opencode-1.3.2 (SessionInfo now has lastActivity + idleTimeoutMinutes)

Two changes:
1. GET /api/sessions — no change needed: listUserSessions already returns the full
   SessionInfo array; the new fields flow through automatically.

2. GET /api/sessions/:hash — currently returns { hash, state, url }.
   Change to also include lastActivity and idleTimeoutMinutes.
   Call getPodState(hash) is insufficient alone — need lastActivity too.
   Options:
   a) Add a new pod-manager helper getSessionInfo(hash, req) that returns full SessionInfo
   b) Keep getPodState but also call a new getLastActivity(hash) helper
   Prefer (a): add export getSessionInfo(hash: string, req): Promise<SessionInfo | null>
   to pod-manager that reads the pod (or PVC if pod absent) and returns full info.
   Return 404 if neither PVC nor pod exists.

title: Commit
description: Ensure code quality and documentation accuracy through systematic cleanup and review. **STEP 1: Code Cleanup** Systematically clean up development artifacts: 1. **Remove Debug Output**: Search for and remove all temporary debug output statements used during development.    Look for language-specific debug output methods (console logging, print statements, debug output functions).    Remove any debugging statements that were added for development purposes.  2. **Review TODO/FIXME Comments**:    - Address each TODO/FIXME comment by either implementing the solution or documenting why it's deferred    - Remove completed TODOs    - Convert remaining TODOs to proper issue tracking if needed  3. **Remove Debugging Code Blocks**:    - Remove temporary debugging code, test code blocks, and commented-out code    - Clean up any experimental code that's no longer needed    - Ensure proper error handling replaces temporary debug logging  **STEP 2: Documentation Review** Review and update documentation to reflect final implementation: 1. **Update Long-Term Memory Documents**: Based on what was actually implemented:    - If  exists: Update it if requirements changed during development    - If  exists: Update it if architectural impacts were identified    - If  exists: Update it if design details were refined or changed    - Otherwise: Document any changes in the plan file 2. **Compare Against Implementation**: Review documentation against actual implemented functionality 3. **Update Changed Sections**: Only modify documentation sections that have functional changes 4. **Remove Development Progress**: Remove references to development iterations, progress notes, and temporary decisions 5. **Focus on Final State**: Ensure documentation describes the final implemented state, not the development process 6. **Ask User to Review Document Updates** **STEP 3: Final Validation** - Run existing tests to ensure cleanup didn't break functionality - Verify documentation accuracy with a final review - Ensure code is ready for production/delivery Update task progress and mark completed work as you finalize the feature.

title: opencode: bugfix (development-plan-fix-session-timeout-reset.md)
description: Responsible vibe engineering session using bugfix workflow for opencode

title: Reproduce
description: Gather specific information to reliably reproduce the reported bug: - What are the exact OS, browser/runtime versions, and hardware specs? - What is the precise sequence of actions that trigger the bug? - What error messages, logs, or stack traces are available? - Does this happen every time or intermittently? - How many users are affected and what is the business impact?  Create test cases that demonstrate the problem. Document your findings and create tasks as needed.

title: Write failing test: WS activity not refreshed during open connection
Failing test: pod killed despite recent session activity in opencode instance
Failing test: deleteIdlePods kills pod despite recent opencode session activity
Failing test: listUserSessions returns stale lastActivity for active pod
Analyze
description: Examine the code paths involved in the bug, identify the root cause, and understand why the issue occurs. Use debugging tools, add logging, and trace through the problematic code. Document your analysis and create tasks as needed.

title: Fix
description: Implement the solution based on your analysis:  - If  exists: Follow the design from it - Otherwise: Elaborate design options and present them to the user  Before implementing, assess the approach: - How critical is this system? What is the blast radius if the fix causes issues? - Should this be a minimal fix or a more comprehensive solution?  Make targeted changes that address the root cause without introducing new issues. Be careful to maintain existing functionality while fixing the bug.

title: Verify
description: Test the fix thoroughly to ensure the original bug is resolved and no new issues were introduced. Run existing tests, create new ones if needed, and verify the solution is robust.

title: Finalize
description: Ensure code quality and documentation accuracy through systematic cleanup and review.  **STEP 1: Code Cleanup** Systematically clean up development artifacts: - Remove all temporary debug output statements used during bug investigation (console logging, print statements, debug output functions) - Address each TODO/FIXME comment by either implementing the solution or documenting why it's deferred - Remove completed TODOs and convert remaining ones to proper issue tracking if needed - Remove temporary debugging code, test code blocks, and commented-out code - Ensure proper error handling replaces temporary debug logging  **STEP 2: Documentation Review** Review and update documentation to reflect the bug fix: - If  exists, update it if design details were refined or changed during the fix - Compare documentation against the actual bug fix implementation - Update only the documentation sections that have functional changes - Remove references to investigation iterations, progress notes, and temporary decisions - Ensure documentation describes the final fixed state, not the debugging process - Ask the user to review document updates  **STEP 3: Final Validation** - Run existing tests to ensure cleanup didn't break functionality - Verify documentation accuracy with a final review - Ensure bug fix is ready for production - Update task progress and mark completed work as you finalize the bug fix

title: Explore

Document your findings and create tasks as needed.

Consider edge cases, dependencies, and potential challenges.  - If architectural changes needed and  exists: Document in  - Otherwise: Create tasks to track architectural decisions  - If  exists: Adhere to the design in it - Otherwise: Elaborate design options and present them to the user  Document the planning work thoroughly and create implementation tasks as part of the code phase as needed.

title: Add new i18n keys for two-panel layout (en.ts + de.ts)
Update LoadingScreen: accept optional initialMessage prop, append to redirect URL
Rewrite e2e tests (session-lifecycle.spec.ts) for new two-panel UI selectors
Add opencode web UI: read ?initialMessage on mount and auto-send first message
Delete setup-form.tsx after logic is inlined into app.tsx
Add router API unit test: POST /api/sessions with initialMessage appended to url
ensureBootstrapConfigMap: create per-session ConfigMap with initial-message.txt when initialMessage is provided
Update ensurePVC: store ANNOTATION_INITIAL_MESSAGE on PVC for durable display in listSessions
Update ensurePod: mount bootstrap ConfigMap + update startup script to run opencode run once via sentinel
Update terminateSession: delete bootstrap ConfigMap on teardown
Add k8s readiness probe to pod spec (httpGet /health); update getPodState to check Ready condition
Rewrite app.tsx: two-panel layout shell (sidebar + main, collapse signal)
Update getSessionState + listUserSessions: query pod session API to build deep-link URL ({pod_url}/{base64dir}/session/{id})
Add router API unit tests: bootstrap ConfigMap creation, initialMessage in PVC annotation, deep-link URL in getSessionState response
Build sidebar: header (Logo + collapse button), New Session button, Recents collapsible with session items + 3-dot menu
Build main area: welcome header + sessions card with status dots, open/resume click handlers

Typecheck + smoke test full two-panel app

Code
description: Follow your plan to build the solution:  - If  exists: Follow the design from it - Otherwise: Elaborate design options and present them to the user - If  exists: Build according to the architecture from it - Otherwise: Elaborate architectural options and present them to the user - If  exists: Ensure requirements from it are met - Otherwise: Ensure existing requirements are met based on your task context  Write clean, well-structured code with proper error handling. Prevent regression by building, linting, and executing existing tests. Stay flexible and adapt the plan as you learn more during implementation. Update task progress and create new tasks as needed.

title: packages/opencode-router: ensureBootstrapConfigMap — new function that creates per-session ConfigMap "opencode-bootstrap-{hash}" with data key "initial-message.txt" = initialMessage. Only creates if initialMessage is non-empty. Idempotent (ignore Conflict). Pattern: mirror ensureGithubTokenSecret in pod-manager.ts. Also add bootstrapConfigMapName(hash) helper. Ref: plan §"bootstrap ConfigMap + opencode run" decision, task 6.2.15.

packages/opencode-router-app app.tsx: rewrite as two-panel layout shell. flex h-dvh, collapsible <aside> + <main>. Signals: sidebarCollapsed, appPhase(loading|ready|creating|error), sessions[], email, terminating set, form signals (repoUrl/sourceBranch/sessionBranch/promptText). loadSessions polls 5s in ready/loading only. Phase "creating" renders LoadingScreen in main area. Ref: plan §Signal architecture + §Phase mapping, task 6.2.2.

packages/opencode-router-app sidebar component: Logo + collapse button, New Session button (focuses input bar), Recents <Collapsible>. SessionSidebarItem: StatusDot (green/grey/spinner) + branch title (truncated) + hover DropdownMenu. Running=open deeplink, Stopped=resume, 3-dot=terminate+confirm/resume. Ref: plan §Session interaction design + §Status dot, task 6.2.3.

packages/opencode-router-app main area: welcome header (Logo + h1 welcomeBack with email), sessions <h2> card. Session list items: StatusDot + branch title + description subtitle + repo (stripped) + relative age + arrow. Running=open deeplink, Stopped=resume→LoadingScreen. No PR card. Error: inline banner. Ref: plan §Session display fields + §Session interaction design, task 6.2.4.

packages/opencode-router-app session input bar: always-visible bottom bar in <main>. Fields: repoUrl TextField (GIT_URL_PATTERN, blur→suggestBranch), sourceBranch TextField, promptText textarea (required). Read-only sessionBranch display. Send button: disabled unless all 4 signals valid + !submitting. Submit: buildSessionKey validate → createSession(repoUrl, sessionBranch, sourceBranch, promptText) → setAppPhase creating. Ref: plan §Send button condition + §Input bar, task 6.2.5.

packages/opencode-router-app creating phase: when appPhase.kind==="creating", render LoadingScreen in main area (sidebar stays visible). Pass hash + url (deep link from createSession response). LoadingScreen already polls getSessionState and redirects — no change to loading-screen.tsx needed. Ref: plan §Phase machine mapping, task 6.2.6.

packages/opencode-router-app: delete setup-form.tsx. All logic now lives in app.tsx (form signals) + session-input-bar component (6.3.13). GIT_URL_PATTERN re-declared in app.tsx. buildSessionKey imported directly from setup-form-utils.ts. Verify no other imports of setup-form.tsx remain. Ref: plan §Decisions NOT requiring new tasks, task 6.2.13.

packages/opencode-router-app e2e: rewrite session-lifecycle.spec.ts for new two-panel UI. goHome waits for "Welcome back". newSession: fill repoUrl + Tab + fill sourceBranch + fill prompt → click send. Terminate: hover session → open 3-dot → click Terminate. Resume: hover session → 3-dot → Resume OR click stopped row. autocapitalize test: check input bar fields. Ref: plan §E2E test rewrite scope, task 6.2.11.

packages/opencode-router api.test.ts: add unit tests for (1) POST /api/sessions with initialMessage → ensureBootstrapConfigMap called with correct args; (2) ANNOTATION_INITIAL_MESSAGE present in PVC ensurePVC call; (3) GET /api/sessions/:hash returns deep-link url when pod ready + session found (mock pod API). Ref: plan §deep link URL + §bootstrap ConfigMap, task 6.2.21.
packages/opencode-router-app: run bun typecheck from packages/opencode-router-app and packages/opencode-router. Fix all type errors. Verify app builds with bun run build. Ref: task 6.2.7.
fix(pod-manager): use 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost in startup health check + attach URL to avoid IPv6 issue

packages/opencode-router: ensurePVC — add ANNOTATION_INITIAL_MESSAGE = initialMessage to PVC annotations (alongside existing ANNOTATION_USER_EMAIL etc). Update SessionKey type to include initialMessage?: string. Update listUserSessions to read ANNOTATION_INITIAL_MESSAGE from PVC ann and include as description?: string on returned SessionInfo. Update SessionInfo type. Ref: plan §"Session display fields", task 6.2.16.
fix LoadingScreen: only redirect when URL is deep link; fallback to base URL after 10 polls
fix session click: if running session URL is not deep link, show LoadingScreen instead of direct navigate
shared SessionItem component: repo+age+description (compact sidebar variant + full list variant)
app.tsx: add "open" phase — render iframe in main, keep sidebar visible, highlight active session
session-input-bar: fix send button styling + disabled state label
pod-manager: replace opencode run bootstrap with ?prompt= URL redirect — remove ConfigMap, sentinel, opencode run; append ?prompt= to session URL when no sessions exist yet

packages/opencode-router pod-manager: rewrite ensurePod startup + readiness probe. Serve in background, wget-loop /health, fire opencode run --attach + sentinel. Add readinessProbe httpGet /health. Add bootstrap ConfigMap volume/mount when initialMessage present. Ref: plan startup script + readiness probe sections, tasks 6.2.17+6.2.19.

packages/opencode-router pod-manager: getPodState — update to check k8s Ready condition (pod.status.conditions find type=Ready status=True) instead of phase=Running. Ensures LoadingScreen waits until opencode serve is actually healthy. Ref: plan §readiness probe, task 6.2.19.

packages/opencode-router pod-manager: terminateSession — delete bootstrap ConfigMap "opencode-bootstrap-{hash}" on teardown (ignore NotFound). Pattern: mirror github token secret deletion on lines 589-592 of pod-manager.ts. Ref: plan §bootstrap ConfigMap, task 6.2.18.

packages/opencode-router pod-manager: deep link URL — new helper deepLinkUrl(podUrl, sessionId). base64 = Buffer.from("/home/opencode/repo").toString("base64").replace(/=+$/,""). Update listUserSessions: for running pods, fetch GET http://{podIP}:{port}/session?limit=1&roots=true → if data[0].id exists, use deepLinkUrl. Update GET /api/sessions/:hash handler: once pod ready + session found, return deepLinkUrl in url field. Ref: plan §Deep link URL, task 6.2.20.

packages/opencode-router api.ts: POST /api/sessions — read optional initialMessage from request body, pass to ensurePVC + ensureBootstrapConfigMap + ensurePod via SessionKey. No validation needed (optional). Ref: plan §initialMessage flow, tasks 6.2.15–6.2.17.

packages/opencode-router-app api.ts: extend createSession(repoUrl, branch, sourceBranch, initialMessage?: string) — pass initialMessage in POST body. Update Session interface: add description?: string. No other changes to api.ts. Ref: plan §Signal architecture, task 6.2.9.

packages/opencode-router-app i18n: add new keys to en.ts + de.ts. Keys: app.welcomeBack, app.sessions, app.recents, app.newSession.repoUrl.placeholder, app.newSession.sourceBranch.placeholder, app.newSession.prompt.placeholder, sidebar.collapse, sidebar.expand, session.state.ready, session.state.inactive, session.action.open, session.action.openInNewTab. Keep all existing keys. Ref: plan §i18n keys, task 6.2.1.
Commit
description: Ensure code quality and documentation accuracy through systematic cleanup and review. **STEP 1: Code Cleanup** Systematically clean up development artifacts: 1. **Remove Debug Output**: Search for and remove all temporary debug output statements used during development.    Look for language-specific debug output methods (console logging, print statements, debug output functions).    Remove any debugging statements that were added for development purposes.  2. **Review TODO/FIXME Comments**:    - Address each TODO/FIXME comment by either implementing the solution or documenting why it's deferred    - Remove completed TODOs    - Convert remaining TODOs to proper issue tracking if needed  3. **Remove Debugging Code Blocks**:    - Remove temporary debugging code, test code blocks, and commented-out code    - Clean up any experimental code that's no longer needed    - Ensure proper error handling replaces temporary debug logging  **STEP 2: Documentation Review** Review and update documentation to reflect final implementation: 1. **Update Long-Term Memory Documents**: Based on what was actually implemented:    - If  exists: Update it if requirements changed during development    - If  exists: Update it if architectural impacts were identified    - If  exists: Update it if design details were refined or changed    - Otherwise: Document any changes in the plan file 2. **Compare Against Implementation**: Review documentation against actual implemented functionality 3. **Update Changed Sections**: Only modify documentation sections that have functional changes 4. **Remove Development Progress**: Remove references to development iterations, progress notes, and temporary decisions 5. **Focus on Final State**: Ensure documentation describes the final implemented state, not the development process 6. **Ask User to Review Document Updates** **STEP 3: Final Validation** - Run existing tests to ensure cleanup didn't break functionality - Verify documentation accuracy with a final review - Ensure code is ready for production/delivery Update task progress and mark completed work as you finalize the feature.
